marketMood: Higher-for-longer shock fades as markets reopen Monday; Marvell's S&P 500 debut highlights AI infrastructure momentum even as Fed rate-hike signals, US-Iran peace fragility, and oil volatility keep bulls cautious.

upcomingEvents.event: Fed bank stress test results; Micron (MU), Paychex (PAYX), Jefferies (JEF) earnings; May new home sales
May PCE prices, Q1 GDP final estimate, May durable goods orders; Darden (DRI) earnings
Pentagon defense contracting ban on Alibaba, Baidu, BYD and 185 other Chinese military-designated firms takes effect

topStories.headline: Fed Holds Under Chair Warsh, Signals Possible Rate Hike in 2026 — Markets Try to Recover
topStories.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting kept rates at 3.5–3.75% but 9 of 18 policymakers now project at least one hike by year-end, reversing earlier expectations of multiple cuts.
topStories.marketImpact: S&P 500 ↓1.21%, Dow ↓507 pts, Nasdaq ↓1.34% on Friday; 2Y Treasury yield ↑ 16bps to 4.21%; DXY hit 1-year high ~100.7. Futures bounce +0.46% Monday but higher-for-longer overhang pressures rate-sensitive growth stocks ↓

topStories.headline: Marvell Technology Joins S&P 500 Today, Up 265% in 2026
topStories.summary: MRVL officially enters the S&P 500 in the quarterly rebalance, replacing Pool Corp and Campbell's, after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang called it a potential next trillion-dollar company.
topStories.marketImpact: MRVL ↑ on index inclusion passive buying; stock up ~265% YTD. Broader semiconductor ETF SOXX rallied ~6% last week on AI infrastructure momentum ↑

topStories.headline: US-Iran Nuclear Talks in Switzerland Abruptly Canceled
topStories.summary: VP JD Vance canceled his Switzerland trip as Iran reportedly demanded a halt to Israeli operations in Lebanon before technical post-MOU talks could begin.
topStories.marketImpact: Oil ↑ briefly on ceasefire fragility; WTI ~$77/barrel but geopolitical risk premium returns to watch. Energy sector on edge as Strait of Hormuz traffic recovery could stall — watch XLE ↑

topStories.headline: Israel-Hezbollah Ceasefire Renewed After June Flare-Up; Oil Tumbles ~10% for the Week
topStories.summary: A renewed ceasefire was brokered after Israel escalated southern Lebanon operations; the Strait of Hormuz has partially reopened with over 12.5M barrels shipped Wednesday.
topStories.marketImpact: WTI oil ↓ ~10% for the week from $89–93 spike back to ~$77; gold ↓ to $4,150/oz (3rd weekly decline) as stronger dollar weighs. Goldman Sachs cut year-end gold target to $4,900 from $5,400 ↓

topStories.headline: Amazon Signs Multibillion-Dollar AI Fiber Optic Deal with Corning
topStories.summary: Amazon will source optical fiber, cable, and connectivity from Corning for its US data-center expansion, creating 1,000 advanced manufacturing jobs in North Carolina — the latest in Corning's run of AI mega-deals.
topStories.marketImpact: GLW ↑4% on announcement; AMZN reinforces domestic AI supply chain strategy. Optical fiber flagged as a critical and undersupplied chokepoint in AI infrastructure ↑

topStories.headline: Pentagon Labels Alibaba, Baidu, BYD as Chinese Military Companies
topStories.summary: The DoD designated 188 Chinese companies including Alibaba, Baidu, and BYD on its expanded military-company list; a defense contracting ban kicks in June 30 with a broader third-party procurement ban following in June 2027.
topStories.marketImpact: BABA, BIDU ↓ on designation; US-China tech tensions escalate. China's MOFCOM warned of 'resolute and forceful retaliation.' Watch for retaliatory measures on US firms with China exposure ↓

companiesToWatch.name: Marvell Technology
companiesToWatch.reason: S&P 500 inclusion effective today triggers forced passive buying; Jensen Huang's 'next trillion-dollar company' endorsement and 265% YTD gain in focus.

companiesToWatch.name: Corning Inc.
companiesToWatch.reason: AI fiber infrastructure beneficiary with Amazon, Nvidia, and Meta supply deals in hand; optical fiber supply-demand imbalance persists as data-center buildout accelerates.

companiesToWatch.name: FedEx
companiesToWatch.reason: Reports earnings Tuesday June 23; market watching for macro freight demand signal and cost-discipline progress amid rate-hike uncertainty.

companiesToWatch.name: Micron Technology
companiesToWatch.reason: Reports earnings Wednesday June 24 — key read on HBM and AI memory demand versus supply normalization in the semiconductor cycle.

companiesToWatch.name: Alibaba Group
companiesToWatch.reason: Pentagon military-company designation with defense contracting ban effective June 30; broader US-China tech friction headwinds compound existing regulatory pressures.

latestPicks.thesis: Laser-source chokepoint in silicon photonics/CPO at ~$290M cap; won qualification orders from leading transceiver vendor — described as 'early $AAOI stage' ahead of volume ramp
CPO-adjacent name rotated into after trimming Lumentum/Coherent profits; positioned for co-packaged optics adoption ramp in AI data centers

priorPickMoves.move: Serenity signaled profit-taking on Lumentum in early June after prior gains; rotated proceeds into CPO names SIVSF and Foci
Coherent similarly trimmed as part of the same early-June CPO rotation — both LITE and COHR had been major 2026 beneficiaries of the photonics thesis

serenity.sentiment: Strong retail following (127k+ on X); self-reported +3,612% YTD as of June 11 (unaudited, leveraged, volatile). Growing mainstream press coverage. No specific posts from June 19–22 confirmed via web search — check X feed directly: https://x.com/aleabitoreddit

tickerDescriptions.SPY: SPDR S&P 500 ETF tracking the 500 largest US publicly traded companies
tickerDescriptions.QQQ: Invesco QQQ ETF tracking the Nasdaq-100 index of the largest non-financial Nasdaq-listed companies
tickerDescriptions.TLT: iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF, a proxy for long-duration US government bond prices
tickerDescriptions.MRVL: Marvell Technology Inc., fabless semiconductor company specializing in AI data center chips, custom ASICs, and silicon photonics — joining S&P 500 June 22
tickerDescriptions.SOXX: iShares Semiconductor ETF tracking the Philadelphia Stock Exchange Semiconductor Sector Index
tickerDescriptions.XLE: Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF tracking US energy companies including oil majors and E&P firms
tickerDescriptions.USO: United States Oil Fund ETF tracking WTI crude oil futures
tickerDescriptions.XOM: Exxon Mobil Corporation, the largest US integrated oil and gas major
tickerDescriptions.GLD: SPDR Gold Shares ETF tracking the price of physical gold bullion
tickerDescriptions.AMZN: Amazon.com Inc., e-commerce and cloud computing giant via AWS, expanding heavily into AI data center infrastructure
tickerDescriptions.GLW: Corning Incorporated, specialty glass and optical fiber manufacturer at the center of AI data center infrastructure deals with Amazon, Nvidia, and Meta
tickerDescriptions.BABA: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd., Chinese e-commerce and cloud computing conglomerate listed as NYSE ADR
tickerDescriptions.BIDU: Baidu Inc., Chinese internet search and AI company listed as NASDAQ ADR
tickerDescriptions.FXI: iShares China Large-Cap ETF tracking the 50 largest Chinese companies listed in Hong Kong
tickerDescriptions.FDX: FedEx Corporation, global logistics and freight delivery company reporting Q4 earnings June 23
tickerDescriptions.MU: Micron Technology Inc., leading US manufacturer of DRAM and NAND memory chips including high-bandwidth memory (HBM) for AI workloads
tickerDescriptions.LITE: Lumentum Holdings Inc., optical and photonic products manufacturer serving datacom and telecom markets
tickerDescriptions.COHR: Coherent Corp., manufacturer of optical fiber components and laser technology for AI data center interconnects

quickHits: Japan May exports rose 17.0% YoY, beating the 16.2% estimate — tech components, autos, and nonferrous metals led (Nikkei Asia)
Goldman Sachs cuts year-end gold price target to $4,900/oz from $5,400 following hawkish Fed repricing and stronger dollar (Bloomberg)
Flex Ltd also joins the S&P 500 today alongside Marvell in the quarterly rebalance, replacing Campbell's (S&P Dow Jones Indices)
Cerebras Systems (CRBS) up 3% as multiple Wall Street firms initiate coverage on the AI chip startup (CNBC)
